title: NBA Daily Rundown
subtitle: May 11, 2026 – Playoff Action Heats Up & Lottery Shakes Future

items.headline: Wizards Win No. 1 Pick in NBA Draft Lottery, Clippers Land Pacers' No. 5!
items.summary: The Washington Wizards secured the coveted No. 1 overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft Lottery on Sunday, May 10, marking a significant step in their rebuilding efforts. The Los Angeles Clippers were also big winners, as the Indiana Pacers' No. 5 pick conveyed to them as part of a previous trade for Ivica Zubac, a 'brutal outcome' for Indiana. The Chicago Bulls jumped to No. 4, while the Milwaukee Bucks will pick 10th.

items.headline: Knicks Sweep Sixers, Advance to Eastern Conference Finals
items.summary: The New York Knicks dominated the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 4, securing a 144-114 victory and a 4-0 series sweep to advance to the Eastern Conference Finals. New York tied the NBA playoff record with 25 three-pointers in a historic offensive display.

items.headline: Timberwolves Even Series with Spurs After Wembanyama Ejection
items.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the San Antonio Spurs 114-109 in Game 4, evening their Western Conference Semifinals series at 2-2. Victor Wembanyama was ejected in the second quarter after being assessed a Flagrant 2 foul.

items.headline: Cavs Take Game 3 Against Pistons
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers beat the Detroit Pistons 116-109 in Game 3 of their series. Detroit still leads the series 2-1.

items.headline: Thunder Dominate Lakers, Up 3-0 in Series
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder continued their impressive playoff run, defeating the Los Angeles Lakers 131-108 in Game 3 to take a commanding 3-0 series lead.

items.headline: Pistons at Cavaliers - Game 4
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons look to extend their series lead against the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 4 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals.

items.headline: Thunder at Lakers - Game 4
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder aim to close out their series against the Los Angeles Lakers in Game 4.

items.headline: McBride, Brunson Lead Knicks' Record-Breaking Offense
items.statContext: Stepped up big in series-clinching Game 4.

items.headline: Cunningham Records Triple-Double in Cavs Loss
items.statContext: Impressive all-around performance despite Game 3 loss.

items.headline: Edwards Powers Wolves to Tie Series
items.statContext: Led all scorers in crucial Game 4 win against Spurs.

items.headline: Deni Avdija: Blazers' All-Star Cornerstone
items.statContext: Israeli forward's breakout All-Star campaign establishes him as a franchise cornerstone for Portland.

items.headline: Towns Double-Double in Knicks' Sweep
items.statContext: Contributed a double-double in the Knicks' Game 4 victory.

items.headline: NBA Draft Lottery Reshapes Future for Several Teams
items.summary: Beyond the Washington Wizards' No. 1 pick, the 2026 NBA Draft Lottery had major implications, particularly for the Indiana Pacers, who saw their No. 5 pick convey to the Los Angeles Clippers due to the Ivica Zubac trade. This outcome has been termed a 'major win' for the Clippers and a 'nightmare scenario' for the Pacers.

items.headline: Star Player Injury Updates
items.summary: Knicks F OG Anunoby was out for Game 4 (May 10) due to a hamstring injury. 76ers C Joel Embiid was day-to-day for Game 4 (May 10) with a hip injury. Pistons G Kevin Huerter is out for Game 4 (May 11) with an adductor injury, and Caris LeVert (DET) is expected to return today from a heel contusion.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Contract Extension Talks Loom for Trail Blazers
items.summary: Following his All-Star season, Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija's contract situation is a key storyline. While under contract for two more seasons, Avdija becomes eligible for an extension in October 2026, with his current team-friendly deal making a long-term commitment a priority for Portland.
